programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: This week comes from Sutton Coldfield. French business analyst Laetitia Kotsiopoulos plans to wow her guests - Greek mum Eva Philippou, publican Randy Johnson, lettings agent Sharon Wozniak and hairdresser Patrick McMurray.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In Sutton Coldfield Eva Philippou hopes a big fat Greek dinner will score her top marks, and has some dancing in store for her entertainment. Hairdresser Patrick has a lot to say for himself but has publican Randy already heard enough from him?

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In Sutton Coldfield, publican Randy Johnson wants to prove he can put on more than bog-standard pub grub. Randy tries to impress with a black tie theme and some interesting culinary techniques, including cooking with house bricks.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In Sutton Coldfield lettings agent Sharon Wozniak plans to impress her guests with a classic Birmingham curry, but some of them seem unimpressed with her menu. Sharon manages to get French foodie Laetitia to admit that she served good local wine.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: On the last night in Sutton Coldfield, outspoken hairdresser Patrick McMurray goes all out to win the cash prize. With a little help from his wife, Patrick prepares a classic beef Wellington, but will his fillet steak impress French foodie Laetitia?

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: 30-year-old childrens food educator Beth Stephens from Suffolk hosts the first dinner of the week.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Its the second night in Lowestoft, and caravan park owner Ray is hoping to win the thousand pound cash prize with a Spanish-themed evening.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: The third evening of this competition is being hosted by luxury-loving beautician Polly, who is determined to show that she is more than just a pretty face.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Its the fourth night in Lowestoft, where host Albanian born civil servant Jonalt is hoping to win the prize with his menu of Albanian family recipes.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Its the fifth and final night of this new competition in Suffolk, and ASBO officer Rachel is hoping to win her guests over with an evening of comfort food.

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In Lancashire, love is in the air for Rosie and Perfect Dave, newlyweds Melisa and Kyle cook together for the very first time, and theres a football revelation on Linda and Garys night.

programme.title: George Clarkes Old House, New Home
programme.desc: George tackles a cottage conundrum, where the only bathroom is through the only bedroom. He also meets a couple who have ambitious plans to turn their loft into a luxurious cinema room.

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Tracey and Iain from south Wales are looking to buy a holiday home in Ibiza. Theyre drawn to the pretty town of Santa Eularia so theyre calling upon the skills of expert Ben Hillman.

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Keen cyclists Andy and Jacqui have decided that now is the time to find a Spanish holiday home that they and their five children can enjoy. These outdoor enthusiasts want to be within walking distance of the beach, and have a budget of £75,000.

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Barbara and Butch are searching for the perfect holiday home in Lucca, Italy, for their £90k. Ben Hillman shows them five fantastic options, but will any make them want to Lucc-again?

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Debra and Greg look for a holiday home in the beautiful countryside of Murcia, which one day they hope to make their permanent home. Jasmine Harman shows them what their £150k can buy.

programme.title: Help! I Bought It at Auction with Sarah Beeny
programme.desc: In Herne Bay, Leezac hopes to create a modern home on a modest budget, but a steel beams causing problems. Alice hits a brick wall with her Somerset townhouse.

programme.title: The Disappearance of Jay Slater
programme.desc: Within hours of Jay Slaters disappearance in summer 2024, mass online interest set conspiracy theories flying. This is the exclusive story of Jays death and its extreme aftermath.

programme.title: 24 Hours in Police Custody
programme.desc: The case of the brutal murder of 86-year-old Una Crown in 2013 is re-opened. Police think they know the killer but will the science back them up?

programme.title: 24 Hours in Police Custody
programme.desc: After forensics get a partial DNA match to a previous suspect, officers bring the man in for questioning. After 13 years of denial will he finally confess?

programme.title: The Copenhagen Test

programme.title: Mud Mountain Haulers
programme.desc: Craig and his crew log a burned section of forest north of Kamloops, but the lethal mix of ash and rainwater takes a toll on the machines and the crew. Brent and his crew clear a fire guard around a clients homestead to protect it from wildfires.

programme.title: Mud Mountain Haulers

programme.title: Air Force One
programme.desc: High-altitude thriller in which the presidential aircraft, Air Force One, is hijacked by Russian communist radicals demanding the release of the Kazakhstani leader. With the First Lady and her family trapped on board, the hijackers threaten to kill one hostage every half-hour until their demands are met. What they dont know is that the president himself is hiding in the hold and formulating a plan to save his family.

programme.desc: Dougie Donnelly presents highlights of the Scottish Cup semi-final between Celtic and St. Mirren at Hampden Park. Celtic have reached the final seven times in the past nine seasons and reached this stage with a memorable penalty shootout victory over Rangers at Ibrox in the quarter-finals. St Mirren will take confidence from their surprise victory over Celtic in Decembers League Cup final. And there is a look back at the best moments from yesterdays semi-final between Dunfermline and Falkirk.

programme.title: Yesterday
programme.desc: Jack Malik is a struggling singer-songwriter in an English seaside town whose dreams of fame are rapidly fading, despite the fierce devotion and support of his childhood best friend, Ellie. After a freak bus accident during a mysterious global blackout, Jack wakes up to discover that The Beatles have never existed. Performing songs by the greatest band in history to a world that has never heard them, Jack becomes on overnight sensation with a little help from his agent.

programme.title: BBC Comedy Short Films
programme.desc: Short films showcasing fresh talent and famous faces as youve never seen them before.

programme.title: Behind Bars: Sex, Bribes and Murder
programme.desc: After a three-minute video featuring a female prison officer and an inmate having sex inside HMP Wandsworth goes viral, award-winning journalist Mobeen Azhar begins an investigation into what happened and why. A year of twists, turns and damning revelations pulls back the curtain on a dangerous wider world of coercion, corruption and contraband thriving behind bars.

programme.title: Behind Bars: Sex, Bribes and Murder
programme.desc: As Mobeens investigation deepens, it becomes clearer that illicit relationships between prisoners and jail staff are just the tip of the iceberg. The bigger story involves organised criminal gangs determined to cash in on a lucrative prison drug economy. When news breaks of a prison governor being jailed for a relationship with a drug-dealing gang boss, Mobeen becomes determined to uncover the true extent of criminal influence inside our prisons.

programme.title: Ashley Cain: Into the Danger Zone
programme.desc: Brazils favelas are considered extremely dangerous, making them one of the worlds most deadly places to grow up. As a result of poverty and a lack of government and police control, many young men here resort to drug dealing and gang crime under the control of the drug cartels. In other neighbourhoods, heavily armed criminal militia groups control day-to-day life with an iron grip, extorting residents - fail to pay or cross them, and the consequences can be deadly.

programme.title: Ashley Cain: Into the Danger Zone
programme.desc: South Africa has for years battled the scourge of rhino poaching, fuelled by insatiable demand for their horns in Asia. Ashley embarks on a mission to find out who is behind these brutal acts, discovering how the challenges young men face growing up in deprived rural communities can lead to illegal poaching. Hanging out with men from KwaZulu-Natal - or Zululand - he earns their trust and joins them on a legal hunt for bushmeat in order to understand what day-to-day life is like here.

But he turns the water on too fast, making the hose voosh up into a big snake of water that covers Bing and Sula in wet paint! Flop shows Bing how to turn the tap on slowly.

programme.title: Love Monster
programme.desc: Love Monster and Tiniest Fluffiest have trained together so they can win Three Legged Race Day, but disaster strikes when Elder Kitten puts them into different teams.

programme.title: Roots and Fruits
programme.desc: Live from the Roots and Fruits theatre, its Corn singing an A-maiz-ing Counting Song about his remarkably organised kernel rows.

programme.title: Mojo Swoptops
programme.desc: Betty the ice cream van is so full of ice cream that its making her speed around town out of control. Its up to Mojo and Bo with the Racer Top to stop her and save those treats.

programme.title: Chuggington
programme.desc: The Trainees are on a mission to play top-secret spy games in Chuggington but their sleuthing missions turn into wild shenanigans.

programme.title: Supertato
programme.desc: Follow the adventures of Supertato, the worlds first potato superhero as he tackles the mischievous Evil Pea, in the supermarket aisles.

programme.title: Bing
programme.desc: Bing and Sula have a lovely time in the park clambering around on the trees. At first it is Sula who needs Bings help to climb up, but when Bing climbs a bit too high he gets stuck and then he needs some help himself.

programme.title: Bing
programme.desc: While looking for things to sell in their shop, Bing and Sula find their old friend Mr Rainybow, but his head is completely gone. So they decide to sell him, and after a nice lady buys his stacky bit for her toilet rolls, they look for some more things to sell. But then they find Mr Rainybows head and are sad that they cant get the rest of him back. However, Charlie loves to knock down Mr Rainybows rings.

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: The Squirrels notice that Happy looks and seems different - he is frowning, his smile is upside down, and hes wearing new trainers! When they check in on him, Happy is frustrated, but he cant seem to identify where his frustration is coming from. They turn to Duggee for help, who uses his feeling cards to explain an array of feelings to Happy. All the Squirrels help by relaying their past experiences and the feelings that they evoked.

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: The Squirrels become enchanted when they hear a piece of music from Duggees choir. They quickly learn what makes a choir.

programme.title: Go Jetters
programme.desc: Glitch enlists the help of a sceptical Foz and a friendly yak in order to find a yeti in the Himalayas. But when a snowstorm traps them in a cave on the mountainside, the rest of the Go Jetters must battle the elements to find their friends.

programme.title: Peter Rabbit
programme.sub-title: The Tale of the Wrecked House
programme.desc: When a big storm wrecks the rabbits tree house, piecing it back together leads to big adventure.

programme.title: Octonauts
programme.desc: Captain Barnacles and his niece and nephew Ursa and Orson must rescue walrus pups who are stranded on a runaway ice floe - before it collides with a huge iceberg!

programme.title: Vida the Vet
programme.desc: Popcorn breaks the x-ray machine and does everything he can to fix it before Vida finds out.

programme.title: Mojo Swoptops
programme.desc: Its hard enough for Mojo and Bo to help Jackson get all his sheep back in their pen. But its even harder when the hungry sheep start chewing up Prestons flower festival!

programme.title: Activate with Joe Wicks
programme.desc: Cowabunga. Can you graduate from my tubular Surfer Challenge? Welcome to surf school, where we test your surf skills to the limit. We will be doing runners, climbers and burpees, followed by the Activate.

programme.title: Something Special: Were All Friends
programme.desc: Mr. Tumble makes breakfast for Tumble Ted, while Justin and his friends collect eggs for their breakfast.

programme.title: Bluey
programme.desc: Bluey and Chloe embark on a quest to save their playground kingdom from the Greedy Queen.

programme.title: Bluey
programme.desc: During a friendly game of neighborhood cricket, the dads struggle to bowl Rusty out.

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: Duggee hosts a reunion for the first ever Squirrels he looked after. However, the first ever Squirrels are older now and very similar to the parents. Does that mean when the Squirrels are bigger they will be old too.

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: It is a rainy day in the Clubhouse which means its a perfect day for a board game. The Squirrels are introduced to Duggees favourite game, Planet Zap - Quest for the Power Orb.

programme.title: Piripenguins
programme.desc: The Piripenguins fall under the spell of a magical Merguin, a mermaid-penguin, who changes their personalities. But all is not as it seems.

programme.title: Big Lizard
programme.desc: When a pack of pesky, migrating trilobites overrun the ship, Big Lizard has to use a hidden talent to lure them away. But one little trilobite, Rex, decides to stay.

programme.title: Vegesaurs
programme.desc: During a snowball fight, Ginger and the Pea-Rexes accidentally wake up a Kiwimammuthus from its hibernation and have to tire out the furry beast to get it back to sleep.

programme.title: Go Jetters
programme.desc: Glitchs plans to open a hotel on an unspoilt beach in Thailand go wrong when macaque monkeys steal his Grim-o-freeze gun. It doesnt take long for the trouble to spread across Monkey Bay, so the Go Jetters leap into action to help save the beach.

programme.title: Weasy Family

programme.title: Octonauts: Above and Beyond
programme.desc: The Octonauts expand their exploration beyond the sea - and onto land. With new rides and new friends, they are prepared to protect any habitats and animals at risk on land or under the water.

programme.title: Small Potatoes
programme.desc: The gang sing about the importance of family and friends in this heartfelt ballad.

programme.title: Monster Makes
programme.desc: Rebecca has lost her healthy, fruity snacks. Murphi cant find them either, but he does find a huge portrait of his great grandma with the big eyes. This gives him an eye-dea for a make - a pair of silly eyeglasses! The Mini Monsters make gorgeous-looking muffins - but not from the kind of sponge youd expect. Rebecca gets a call on the Magic Monster Mirror from her aunt, who has lost her ducks - she decides to replace them with a quiet, peaceful model version instead.

programme.title: Big Cook Little Cook
programme.desc: Small is entering the local talent show, but shes forgotten the words to her song. Meanwhile, Mike the music man comes to visit, Small flies off to see how celery grows, and the two chefs serve a delicious risotto drum to their talented guest.

programme.title: Lets Go for a Walk

programme.title: Go Wild

programme.desc: Aneeshwar and Lizzie meet the one-horned rhino and some babirusas.

programme.title: Waffles After School Club

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: The circus has come to town and the Squirrels are very excited until they see that it is a flea circus. It is so tiny! It doesnt seem very impressive until they get to see some of the acts, like Fifi and her daring tightrope act, and the flea cannonball. The Squirrels are eager to get involved, especially when they see what the strong flea can do - now thats impressive!

programme.title: Octonauts
programme.desc: When a gigantic saltwater crocodile wanders into freezing waters, the Octonauts must figure out how to get the predator back to his warm water home - without anyone getting hurt.

programme.title: Vida the Vet
programme.desc: Tidbit worries that a watermelon will grow in his tummy after he swallows a seed.

programme.title: Go Jetters
programme.desc: When the Grimbots build their own Easter Island statue, it collapses and threatens the ancient Moai statues.

programme.title: Rafi the Wishing Wizard
programme.desc: Rafi, Ruby and Finn struggle to score in basketball, so Rafi wishes for a magic ball. At first, its fun, as every shot goes in - even trick throws. But the constant winning becomes boring, and the ball bounces endlessly into the hoop by itself. The trio try to contain it, but it escapes again and again. Finally, they return to playing with the ordinary ball, and Finn sinks a real shot, proving true victory is earned.

programme.title: Yukee
programme.desc: When rapper Bushy T visits the garden, his hip-hop beats may help poor Stripes master Yukees skipping rope and join in the fun.

programme.title: Roots and Fruits

programme.title: Bradley and Barney Walsh: Breaking Dad
programme.desc: Actor Bradley Walsh and his 20-year-old son Barney are hitting the road together, driving over 2000 miles from Los Angeles to New Orleans. They might be father and son but, when it comes to travelling, they are worlds apart, with Bradley keen to relax while Barney wanting to live life a little more on the edge.

programme.title: Bradley and Barney Walsh: Breaking Dad
programme.desc: Bradley and Barney saddle up for a Wild West adventure to Arizona. The trip sees them ride Harleys through a desert ghost town and jet ski by Lake Havasus London Bridge, before becoming real-life horsemen at a Cowboy College.

programme.title: Bradley and Barney Walsh: Breaking Dad
programme.desc: The bandwagon continues to Texas, where they do everything bigger - from driving tanks to experiencing space travel in a centrifuge and even looking for alien life with UFO spotters.

programme.title: Bradley and Barney Walsh: Breaking Dad
programme.desc: The final chapter in Bradley and Barneys voyage sees them visit New Orleans. Brad feeds alligators in the Bayou, has a spiritual MOT by a voodoo priest and gets on board the Natchez jazz Steamboat, before leading a street parade to end an epic trip.

programme.title: Bradley and Barney Walsh: Breaking Dad
programme.desc: Barney Walsh has dragged his dad Bradley off the sofa and back to the USA, for another epic road trip. For the first leg of their latest adventure the Walsh boys are in sunny Florida, and begin in Key West, the home of Bradleys favourite author and the southernmost point of the country.

programme.title: Bradley and Barney Walsh: Breaking Dad
programme.desc: In the Great Smoky Mountains, Bradley and Barney get wet and wild canoeing on the iconic rapids seen in `Deliverance, before going chopping mad with competitive lumberjacks. Finally, they take to the woods to search for a mythical mountain beast.

programme.title: Bradley and Barney Walsh: Breaking Dad
programme.desc: Following a rocky start in Red River Gorge, Barneys got another activity in store - a paranormal one, at the haunted Poasttown Elementary School. The next day, the Walshes wake up in Louisville, the home of Muhammad Ali. There they follow in the footsteps of `The Champ, sparring down at a local boxing ring to work up an appetite. Barney rewards his dad with a well-earned KFC.

programme.title: Bradley and Barney Walsh: Breaking Dad
programme.desc: In Ohio, Brad and Barney head straight for Dayton, the home of aviation. After recreating history in the Wright B Flyer, its Barneys turn to take the wheel - of a plane. The Walshes attend a local rodeo, and Brads quick to realise theyre not just there to spectate: theyll be taking part in `the most dangerous eight seconds in sport. However, after a nasty fall from an angry bull, Brad ends up spending the night in hospital, giving `Breaking Dad a whole new meaning.

programme.title: Bradley and Barney Walsh: Breaking Dad
programme.desc: Its Barneys birthday, and after a pit-stop at the iconic Niagara Falls, the Walshes head to Lake Placid to recreate their very own `Miracle on Ice. As a treat that night, Barney swaps the RV bunks for beds at the Trapp Family Lodge. The next day, armed with crampons and ice picks, Brad and Barney take on an frozen rock face before jumping into sleds with some four-legged friends and riding through a winter wonderland in Vermont.

programme.title: Bradley and Barney Walsh: Breaking Dad
programme.desc: On the final leg of their US road trip, Brad and Barney take rally cars for a spin on the ice, before settling the score at Moose Alley bowling in Maine. To help them with the extreme weather, Barneys arranged for some wilderness training with expert Joel Graves, before an early start as the boys go lobster fishing off Swans Island. Finally, to round off an epic adventure, the duo settle down to a good old sing-song.

programme.desc: Phoebe is reunited with her former singing partner, Leslie, a failed television commercial jingle writer who wants to use Phoebes signature song `Smelly Cat in an advertisement for cat litter. programme.sub-title: The One With the Hypnosis Tape

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With the Tiny T-Shirt

Meanwhile, Monica dates a millionaire, and Joey is cast in a play and clashes with the female lead.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Phoebe brings her annoyingly upbeat boyfriend to Mr and Mrs Gellers 35th wedding anniversary party. Meanwhile, Ross is astounded when his parents ask him and the pregnant Rachel to pretend to be married, in order to avoid a potential scandal.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers

programme.title: Impractical Jokers
programme.desc: The guys compete head-to-head selling their toys to focus groups, team up attaching clothespins to unsuspecting shoppers, and play a game of touching laser pointer dots.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers
programme.desc: The Jokers ask random park goers to watch their grandma while they make a quick errand, then a fake documentary interviews them; the losing Joker gets dressed as a clown and punished by the other Jokers and returning special guest, Eric Andre.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers
programme.desc: The Jokers have an over-the-top reaction to a phone call and offer absurd event tickets to people on the street, and the losing Joker is joined by Alf the Alien.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With the Princess Leia Fantasy
programme.desc: Monica is distraught after her break-up with Richard. Phoebe unsuccessfully tries to raise her spirits, but ultimately its Mr Geller who comforts his daughter. Elsewhere, Chandler and Janice begin seeing each other again and Joey cant stand it. The two plan a day of fun to try to overcome Joeys aversions for Chandlers sake. Meanwhile, Rachel tells Phoebe and Monica about Rosss Princess Leia fantasy.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One Where No Ones Ready
programme.desc: In a race against the clock, Ross tries to get the gang dressed and ready for a black-tie benefit at the museum.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Monica decides the best way to get over Richard is to keep herself busy. She develops a `jam plan, hoping the distraction of cooking will divert her attention. After making jars and jars of jam Monica decides she still wants to have a baby, so she begins searching for a sperm donor, and comes across Joeys file. Elsewhere, Phoebe is mistakenly stalked by a man who thinks shes Ursula, and Chandler receives dating advice.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With the Metaphorical Tunnel
programme.desc: Ross panics when he sees his son with a Barbie doll, and Chandlers fear of commitment surfaces in his relationship with Janice.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Phoebes attempts to introduce her visiting half brother Frank Jr to the delights of city life leave him thinking that she works in a brothel. Meanwhile, Ross lists five famous women that he would like to date. To his surprise, actress Isabella Rossellini, one of the women he rejected while narrowing down his list, pops in to Central Perk.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: When Janice asks whether any of the friends have ever dated one another the gang has a flashback to three years ago (one year before the pilot was supposed to have happened) when everyones lives were beginning to come together. Phoebe moves out of Monicas apartment, Chandler searches for a new roommate - ending up with Joey, Monica bumps into Rachel, and Ross learns that Carol is a lesbian.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With the Race Car Bed
programme.desc: When Monica buys a new bed from the Mattress King, the wrong one is delivered. Meanwhile, Joey sees Janice kissing her husband, and Rachels father clashes with Ross.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With the Giant Poking Device
programme.desc: Chandler and Janice reach a turning point in their relationship, while Monica accidentally injures Ben and tries to hide it from Ross.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: On Thanksgiving, the friends form teams and play American football. Ross and Monica relive their sibling rivalry by going head-to-head as opposing team captains. Rachel is upset that Ross didnt choose her for his team, and Joey tries to outwit his foe Chandler. Meanwhile, the turkey is left stewing while the losing team calls for a rematch.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Rachel wonders if shell ever get another job after she quits Central Perk, Joeys new job selling Christmas trees upsets Phoebe, and Ross accidentally breaks a little girls leg.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One Where Chandler Cant Remember Which Sister

programme.sub-title: The One With All the Jealousy

programme.title: Deal or No Deal
programme.desc: Airing from 2005 to 2009 on NBC, the game show series returns to primetime TV with original host Howie Mandel. A contestant must choose from 26 sealed briefcases containing a marker for various amounts of cash -- from one penny to $1 million. The player then eliminates the remaining 25 cases one by one. The chosen ones are opened and the amount of money inside revealed. After several cases are opened, the player is tempted by the Banker to accept an offer of cash in exchange for not continuing the game and possibly winning a larger sum of money.

programme.title: Gold Rush
programme.desc: The boys are back in Alaska (although Discovery dropped the states mention in the title after the first season) hoping to strike it rich mining for gold. Their first effort was anything but rich and glorious, however, as the crew led by Todd Hoffman and his father, Jack, sunk $250,000 into their dream and came away with just $20,000 worth of gold. The dismal summer was filled with injuries, malfunctioning equipment and constant fighting among the greenhorn miners, yet their serious case of gold fever trumps any talk of giving up and leads to a new operating plan in order to salvage their dream. The series also keeps tabs on the efforts of teenager Parker Schnabel and old-timer Dakota Fred to hit the mother lode at separate sites.

programme.title: Gold Rush
programme.desc: The boys are back in Alaska (although Discovery dropped the states mention in the title after the first season) hoping to strike it rich mining for gold. Their first effort was anything but rich and glorious, however, as the crew led by Todd Hoffman and his father, Jack, sunk $250,000 into their dream and came away with just $20,000 worth of gold. The dismal summer was filled with injuries, malfunctioning equipment and constant fighting among the greenhorn miners, yet their serious case of gold fever trumps any talk of giving up and leads to a new operating plan in order to salvage their dream. The series also keeps tabs on the efforts of teenager Parker Schnabel and old-timer Dakota Fred to hit the mother lode at separate sites.

programme.title: First Love
programme.desc: In Tokyo, a young down on his luck boxer meets his first love, not realising that she is caught up in a drug-smuggling scheme.

programme.title: Dads Army
programme.desc: Near the end of World War II, the Walmington-on-Sea Home Guard platoon deals with a female journalist and a German spy.

programme.title: Tora! Tora! Tora!
programme.desc: A realistic and well-documented film re-enacting the events that led up to the Japanese attack on Pearl Harbor, which plunged the United States into World War II. Boasting Oscar-winning special effects, the film gives both the Japanese and American perspectives on events, and highlights some of the major and minor errors that were made on both sides.

programme.title: Annie
programme.desc: Ever since her parents left her as a baby, little Annie has led a hard-knock life with her mean foster-mother, Miss Hannigan. However, that all changes when hard-nosed billionaire and mayor candidate Will Stacks takes her in on the recommendation of his advisers. Stacks believes that hes Annies guardian angel, but the plucky youngsters confidence and sunny outlook may mean that Annie will save Will instead.

programme.title: Battleship
programme.desc: A fleet of ships is forced to do battle with an armada of unknown origins in order to discover and thwart their destructive goals.

programme.title: Anna
programme.desc: Beneath a Russian womans striking beauty lies a hidden secret that will unleash her indelible strength and skill and lead to her becoming one of the most feared assassins on the planet.

programme.title: Last Night in Soho
programme.desc: An aspiring fashion designer is mysteriously able to enter the 1960s, where she encounters a dazzling wannabe singer. However, the glamour is not all it appears to be, and the dreams of the past start to crack and splinter into something far darker.

programme.title: The Frighteners
programme.desc: `Spiritual clearance expert Frank and his three phantom pals are con artists, but when they hit upon Fairwater, they find that a series of bizarre deaths are turning it into a ghost town - literally.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: This trip, some of Guys favourites are totally homemade from coast to coast. In Manhattan, New York, a real deal chef is cranking out homemade pretzels with a special beer cheese sauce. In Vancouver, British Columbia, the butcher shop turned bakery and diner is serving up standards like burgers and turkey pot pie. And in Santa Barbara, California, the family-run joint scratch making authentic Mexican food.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: On this trip, Guys stepping into some family kitchens. In Vancouver, British Columbia, is a Chinese joint hand-pulling noodles and using age-old family recipes for dishes like beef rolls.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: This trip, Guys cruisin down the path less traveled. In Dallas, Guy turns tex-mex into mex-tex, with savory soups. In Baltimore, local rock star baker Duff Goldman stops by to seek out the secrets of the square pizza. In Santa Barbara, CA, a restaurant turnin turkey into fritters and a business into a family affair.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: This trip Guys rolling out for something new from the grill - and more. In San Antonio, Texas, a 60-year-old drive-in where the owners are putting a Thai twist on classic Texas bar-b-que.

programme.title: Barefoot Contessa
programme.desc: Ina is cooking up a fantastic meal with her friend Antonia. She makes gazpacho with goats cheese croutons, mustard marinated flank steak and Sicilian grilled swordfish.

programme.title: Barefoot Contessa
programme.desc: Ina Gartens sharing pot luck party secrets with local farmers Katie and Amanda, who are throwing a start of season bash.

programme.title: Barefoot Contessa
programme.desc: Inas on a cheese adventure and is sharing her favourite cheese recipes. She makes a show stopping spinach and cheddar souffle.

programme.title: Barefoot Contessa
programme.desc: Today is all about desserts in Inas hall of fame. She makes an incredible frozen mocha mousse, easy French apple galettes and balsamic strawberries.

programme.title: Barefoot Contessa
programme.sub-title: Cooking for Jeffrey: Jeffreys Birthday Dinner
programme.desc: Ina Garten throws an Italian-themed birthday dinner with a big surprise for Jeffrey. To get the party started, theres bruschetta with sauteed chard, followed by roasted Italian meatballs, and a rocket and radicchio salad.

programme.title: Barefoot Contessa
programme.sub-title: Cooking for Jeffrey: Deans for Dinner
programme.desc: Ina throws a celebratory meal for her friend Deborah Berke, who has been named the Dean of the Yale Architecture School. She makes a paella salad, goat cheese platter, English oat crackers and a pitcher of rose sangria.

programme.title: Jamie Oliver: Cooking for Less
programme.desc: Jamie Oliver makes a giant Yorkshire pudding with stuffing and roast veg, homemade gnocchi in a cheesy bacon and pea sauce, spicy kipper kedgeree and easy ice cream, all for under a quid a portion.

programme.title: Jamies Money Saving Meals
programme.desc: Jamie Oliver aims to inspire people to eat better and spend less on their weekly food bills. In this first episode - in which all recipes cost under £1.80 per portion - Jamie cooks up a glorious `mothership roast brisket of beef with all the trimmings. He then turns his leftover beef into an incredible Korean stir-fried rice.

programme.title: Jamies Money Saving Meals
programme.desc: Jamie cooks up a glorious roast half shoulder of pork with all the trimmings, and then turns his leftovers into stunning little dim sum buns. He then knocks up meatloaf with spaghetti, with a great tomato and chilli sauce.

programme.title: James Martins Islands to Highlands
programme.desc: James is joined by Michelin-starred chef Kenny Atkinson, and they set out to sample the delights of Northumberland. They learn how mead is made on Holy Island, before James cooks up a Singapore-inspired crab and lobster dish on a stunning beach, and they both enjoy a Puffin cruise at Cockett Island. Theres a trip to a lobster hatchery too, before James cooks local speciality Singing Hinnies on another breathtaking beach.

programme.title: James Martins Islands to Highlands
programme.desc: James is joined by Michelin-starred chef Lisa Goodwin-Allen for a visit to the Isle of Man during the famous TT race warm-up week. James serves up proper biker food, pork burgers with a spicy apple sauce, while Lisa visits local organic juice producers. James picks up some local Manx kippers and makes a delicious soup, before meeting TT legend John McGuinness. Finally, he treats John and his team to some tasty lamb chops with hispi cabbage and an olive glaze.

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers Go Local
programme.desc: Bobby and Minal have built up a loyal customer base, winning a host of awards along the way, including a prestigious Michelin Bib Gourmand. But now they have their eyes on an even bigger prize, a coveted Michelin star.

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers Go Local
programme.desc: Dave and Si are headed to The Stackpole Inn, an award-winning gastro pub in Pembrokeshire. Owners Gary and Becky first took it on 15 years ago. The couple were joined in 2019 by head chef Matt Waldron, who prides himself on using local ingredients.

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers Go Local
programme.desc: The Bikers are in beautiful Northumberland, heading towards Wall, a small village just north of Hexham. Its an area with a bloody past of marauding Vikings and historic battles.

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers Go West
programme.desc: Dave and Si are starting off their great adventure in Scotland. It was on these Scottish roads that they first became best friends, and theyre now returning to one of the places that holds very special memories for them both, the Isle of Bute.

programme.title: Jamie Oliver: Cooking for Less
programme.desc: Jamie Oliver makes a giant Yorkshire pudding with stuffing and roast veg, homemade gnocchi in a cheesy bacon and pea sauce, spicy kipper kedgeree and easy ice cream, all for under a quid a portion.

programme.title: Jamies Money Saving Meals
programme.desc: Jamie Oliver aims to inspire people to eat better and spend less on their weekly food bills. In this first episode - in which all recipes cost under £1.80 per portion - Jamie cooks up a glorious `mothership roast brisket of beef with all the trimmings. He then turns his leftover beef into an incredible Korean stir-fried rice.

programme.title: Jamies Money Saving Meals
programme.desc: Jamie cooks up a glorious roast half shoulder of pork with all the trimmings, and then turns his leftovers into stunning little dim sum buns. He then knocks up meatloaf with spaghetti, with a great tomato and chilli sauce.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Guys traveling all over the country searching for a taste of the South. In Vancouver, British Columbia, Southern staples are served with a twist, like the pulled pork pancakes with a side of Jack Daniels syrup. In Charleston, South Carolina, the local football legend is cranking out some real deal comfort food with favourites like sweet tea-marinated pork chops and deep-fried hot dogs topped with chili.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: In Dundalk, Maryland, a Greek diner is decked out in neon and serving up family recipes. In Oxnard, California, a joint is baking, brewing, and cooking all in the same restaurant.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: On this trip, Guys teaming up with local legends. Outside Baltimore, Guy and Super Bowl champ Joe Theismann head to the kitchen for all types of tacos.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.sub-title: You Can Only Get It Here
programme.desc: This trip Guys finding funky flavours all over the country. In San Francisco, an Asian/American/Mexican fusion joint doing a Korean spicy pork burrito with homemade kimchee.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: This trip, Guys tasting his way through the day. In Tampa Bay, Florida, authentic Mexican street food for the lunch crowd is served out of bus, crankin out favourites like butternut squash tostadas. In Virginia Beach, Virginia, dinner at this popular seafood joint includes totally fresh oysters Rockefeller. And in San Antonio, Texas, a breakfast joint servin up waffles filled with bacon.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: This trip, in Detroit, Michigan, a pizza joint makes pizza pies with homemade toppings, and a unique crepe-filled lasagna. In Virginia, a beachside breakfast favourite the locals come running to for their citrus orange glazed pancakes and innovative benedicts. And in Chicago, Illinois, the tiny dive servin up flavour in massive proportions, like the melt-in-your-mouth pork belly sandwich.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Guy Fieri visits Americas most interesting and unique restaurants.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: This trip, Guys getting up close to some serious gourmet grub. In Chicago, the brewhouse is serving up beer to go with mussels, a mega brisket sandwich and their version of head cheese.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Grilled cheese sandwiches; lasagna and lamb shanks; chili pepper wontons and firebird chicken.

programme.desc: This trip, Guy goes all animal. In Charleston, South Carolina, the husband and wife duo flying high for all things bird, like a duck club with homemade garlic mayo. programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: On this trip, Guy finds a slice of history all over the country. In Santa Barbara, California, the neighbourhood deli is a favourite with locals and celebrities alike for its pastrami.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: This trip Guys rolling coast to coast for flavour. In Astoria, New York, a dive bar cranking out slow roasted lamb ribs with fresh tzatziki.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: This trip Guys heading all over the country for a taste of the south. In San Francisco, a whisky bar putting their own spin on a po boy with avocado and fresh crab mayo.

programme.title: Man v Food
programme.desc: Casey Webb journeys across America to tackle daunting food challenges and sample iconic regional dishes. Enormous food options create problems for even the most voracious of eaters.

programme.title: James Martins Islands to Highlands
programme.desc: James is joined in Northern Ireland by his good pal and local boy Paul Rankin. They brave the Carrick-a-Rede rope bridge together, then James cooks a tasty fish dish with the dramatic coastline as his backdrop. Theres a fishing trip over to Rathlin Island on the menu too, where James tastes some dishes made from the local kelp. James and Paul then visit the Old Lammas Fair, where they taste some of the delicious seafood.

programme.title: James Martins Islands to Highlands
programme.desc: James returns to Northern Ireland with his old chef buddy Paul Rankin, where theyre exploring the area around Lough Neagh, the biggest freshwater lake in the British Isles. James and Paul discover a wasabi farm, a rarity outside of Japan. Next, Paul visits an old friend, a local honey producer, while James cooks overlooking the dramatic backdrop of Slieve Gullion: the highest point in Armagh.

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: Its Bread Week, and the bakers put their stamp on a classic pizza, get in a twist with a classic pastry and tackle a showstopping Smorgåstårta, a decorative Danish sandwich cake.

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: Its Mexican Week, and the bakers take on the sweet bread, Pan Dulce, and a Mexican street food staple, and make a showstopping version of the milk-soaked Tres Leches cake.

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: Its Dessert Week and the bakers get all steamed up in the Signature, tackle a classic lemon meringue pie, and create a showstopping sponge cake with a hidden surprise.

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: Its Halloween Week and the bakers make apple cake, a smore-ish Technical and a spooky showstopping piñata. Whose bakes will be eerie-sistible enough to earn them star baker?

programme.title: Adam Richman Eats Britain
programme.desc: Adam has a hearty hot pot in Lancashire. He also tries a Brazilian sausage roll, world-famous black pudding and kicking Korean chicken-topped waffle.

programme.title: Adam Richman Eats Britain
programme.desc: Adam tries cheddar in Cheddar. He continues his rural discoveries with a cheesy celeriac bake with leeks that he harvests himself and farm-fresh chocolate milk.

programme.title: Man v Food
programme.desc: Adam Richman travels across the United States to eat the countrys best traditional food. Adam has an encounter with a Sasquatch Hamburger at the Big Foot Lodge in Memphis.

programme.title: Man v Food
programme.desc: Adam Richman faces another tough eating challenge in Columbus when he attempts to eat a two-and-a-half pound Dagwood Sandwich. Will he have enough room for dessert?

programme.title: James Martins Islands to Highlands
programme.desc: James takes a magical trip to the stunning Isle of Skye, where hes joined by top chef and ultimate Scotsman Tony Singh. Tony visits a local bakery which doubles as an art gallery and learns to make the local oatcakes. James is inspired by the amazing local seafood and cooks up a tasty dish using plaice and lovage. Their day comes to a perfect end aboard a boat, where they spot dozens of dolphins, and James cooks up a fresh lobster and langoustine dish with an added Scottish twist.

programme.title: James Martins Islands to Highlands
programme.desc: James visits a place hes always wanted to see - the beautiful Shetland Islands - and they definitely do not disappoint. James gets to hire a vintage car to explore the area, where he learns about traditional fish salting and cooks a tasty vegetable curry at a local social enterprise project.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Guys tasting all kinds of international comfort classics. In Santa Barbara theres an authentic British fish and chips joint known for keeping it real - from the haggis to the toad in the hole.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: In Annapolis, Maryland, two Irish brothers serve up food from their homeland with a twist, like Guinness-fried oysters with homemade coleslaw.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: This trip Guys heading out for some global flavour. In Brighton, Colorado, a German joint specialising in homemade kraut and its own take on a kraut burger.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: This trip Guys rolling in for farm-to-table cooking. In Boulder, Colorado, a college town favourite since the 1920s, where theyre flipping grass-fed beef burgers.

programme.title: Patrick Christys Tonight
programme.desc: Patrick chairs two hours of spirited debate and news making interviews. Top guests tackle the issues that matter, with a sharp take on every story.

programme.title: Patrick Christys Tonight Late Edition
programme.desc: Patrick Christys explore tomorrows news, tonight, including all the major stories making the front pages and full coverage of all the breaking news at home and abroad.

programme.title: Breakfast with Stephen and Anne
programme.desc: The Great British Breakfast continues into the weekend with Stephen Dixon and Anne Diamond. Prepare yourself for the weekend with a full round-up of the weeks news and politics and sport.

programme.title: Saturday Morning Live
programme.desc: Welcome the weekend as we celebrate the countrys triumphs in the Best of British feature, and look at the good news where you are.

programme.title: The Weekend
programme.desc: A lively look at all the latest live and breaking news for the weekend from around the UK with our network of GB News reporters, plus views and debate from a wide range of perspectives.

programme.title: Nana Akua
programme.desc: Nana Akua presents her own unique take on the biggest stories of the day.

programme.title: The Saturday Five
programme.desc: Join our provocative panel to discuss and debate the spikiest stories and craziest controversies of the week.

programme.title: The Saturday Five Extra

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It is the national finals of `Great British Menu. Before cooking starts the eight champions are called to Londons Southbank to get a sight of the prize, the chance to cook at a banquet held at the Palace of Westminster, in celebration of everyday Great Britons honoured by the Queen. It has a huge impact on every one of the chefs, each is determined to do all he can to get a dish to the banquet.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its day two of the national finals, and the eight champions cook their fish courses. This years challenge has been to produce dishes that are a lasting legacy to the New Elizabethan Age. They must showcase the transformation in British cuisine during the Queens historic 64 year reign.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It is main course day, the one course every chef wants to cook at the banquet. Judging the chefs dishes are Matthew Fort, Prue Leith and Oliver Peyton and guest judge John Williams MBE, executive chef of The Ritz.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its the final day of the competition, and the last chance for the eight champions to get one of their dishes to the banquet for Queen Elizabeth lls Great Britons. Judges Matthew Fort, Prue Leith and Oliver Peyton will judge the dessert course and then decide on the banquet menu to be served at the Houses of Parliament. Joining them in the judging chamber is Grace Dent, restaurant critic, journalist and author.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The winning chefs arrive at the Palace of Westminster to cook for the Banquet of Great Britons. The chefs have been selected because their dishes showcase contemporary British cuisine and demonstrate the transformation in British food during the Queens historic reign.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Competing for top spot are Danny Parker, head chef at Michelin starred `House of Tides in Newcastle, who is back to try his luck in the competition again after being knocked out in the regional heat last year following a tense tie-break. Hes taking on two newcomers: Dan Fletcher, protege of Phil Howard, and now head chef at `Fenchurch in the City of London, and David Coulson,who runs `Peace and Loaf in Newcastle, which boasts 2 AA rosettes.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: After a tense starter course the chefs are all aiming for 10s with their unique fish dishes. David is celebrating a personal connection to the brief with his `My Cod I Bet Me Maam Has Seen Some Trotters, a tribute to his mum who has worked as a chiropodist. Dans `For All Those Sleepless Nights pays tribute to nightshift workers from the NHS with some bold but classic flavours while Dannys `Not All Superheroes Wear Capes aims to give a taste of luxury to the hardworking staff.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Dan pays tribute to an icon of modern nursing, Florence Nightingale, while Danny in inspired by the founder of the NHS, Aneurin Bevan. Davids dish is dedicated to the air ambulance staff who saved the life of his fiance, and he even takes a trip to meet one of them.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its the chefs last chance to impress and tensions are high. Dan takes a risk with an unusual combination of ewes milk and bergamot while Danny is determined to pull out all the stops with his complex dessert `Aunty Gwens Chocolate. David hopes to transform a childhood comfort classic into a show stopping dessert. Dan also takes a trip to meet Dr Ben Clarke who was working in the Royal London Hospital AandE during the London Bridge terror attack.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two remaining North East chefs must cook their four course menus again, but will they take on board their veterans advice as they serve their `Feast To Say Thank You?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Banquet champion Selin Kiazim, a rising star of the London scene renowned for her Turkish Cypriot fusion flavours, is back hoping to join the elite group of chefs who have made it to the banquet twice. She is up against James Cochran whos worked at some of Londons toughest kitchens including 2 Michelin starred `The Ledbury and Scott Goss from `The Twenty Six in Tunbridge Wells, renowned for his use of local Kent ingredients.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The chefs do battle for the fish course and the atmosphere in the kitchen is tense. Lobster is catch of the day for James whose dish is Caribbean-inspired. Scott is attempting to elevate a classic fish pie, dedicated to his mum who worked as a pharmacist in the NHS for 23 years. And Selin is taking a risk with a complex three-part fish dish featuring sardines, scallops and langoustines, as a nod to the purported health benefits of a Mediterranean diet.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Scott is showcasing rare breed Tamworth pork to say thank you to the rare breed of people who work for the NHS. Selin is serving a complex duck dish using ingredients from all around the world to honour `Our Global NHS. And James is presenting five cuts of goat cooked in five different ways, for his bold dish `Under The Knife.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Selin puts her own unique twist on tea and toast. James creates a decadent coconut parfait accompanied by mango puree filled doughnuts for his dessert `Tree of Life inspired by the coconut palm tree, the NHS and both of their contributions to our health. Scott hopes his elevated gypsy tart `Honey Bee Good will secure him a place in the final.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two remaining London and the South East chefs must cook their four-course menus again, but will they take on board their veterans advice as they re-make their `Feast To Say Thank You?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Vying to be crowned champion of Scotland are three talented newcomers to the GBM kitchen. Lorna McNee is a former Scottish Chef of the Year and is renowned for her technical precision which she has gained from working her entire career at 2 Michelin-starred restaurant Andrew Fairlie at Gleneagles.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its the fish course and the chefs are all out to impress. The heat is increased as all three serve their unique takes on lobster. Ross is hoping to snatch victory with his dish `Lobster Aid. Lornas `Live Well, Live Very Well features only three elements, each of which she needs to deliver perfection.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Ross is hoping to impress with `Oh Deer Its Time For Your Penicillin, his tribute to Scottish scientist Alexander Fleming featuring an unusual yeast element as well as venison. Across the kitchen, Lorna, a former Game Chef of the Year, is also cooking venison in a bid to impress.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its dessert day and the last chance for the chefs to impress before one is sent home. Ross is attempting to elevate a comfort classic into a dish worthy of the banquet. Lornas cooking a personal tribute to the NHS staff who have cared for her during treatment for a rare blood condition, using unusual yuzu and tonka bean flavours. Once again Ben is serving an experimental dish with his `Dance for the Greater Good which includes a bees wax ice cream.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its judging day and the two remaining chefs must impress the panel of Oliver Peyton, Andi Oliver and Matthew Fort. Both chefs are gunning for success and are doing everything they can to deliver their menus to perfection.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Three chefs compete in the heat for the central region. Returner Ryan Simson-Trotman, who failed to reach the national finals last year, is originally from Nuneaton and opened his own restaurant Orwells in the Oxfordshire countryside with his partner Liam, who is also a contestant in the North West region.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its the fish course and the chefs are pulling out all the stops. Marianne pays tribute to the `angels in her local hospital who looked after her during a recent illness. Ryans dish is a reference to the purported health benefits of fish high in omega 3 oils. While Sabrina is inspired by her aunt, a retired nurse who used to take in food to share with her fellow workers during her shifts.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The chefs cook their mains and following yesterdays results, tension is high. Both Marianne and Ryan are cooking muntjac in the hope of impressing the veteran judge, while Sabrina is making an ambitious technical take on a family feast.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: With one dish left to impress the veteran judge, the chefs cook their desserts. Marianne is looking to raise a smile with her `carry on themed dessert, complete with surprise presentation. Sabrina hopes to make it through with an elevated take on jelly and ice cream while Ryan goes all out with his very sweet treat `A Spoonful of Sugar.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two remaining central region chefs do battle for a place in the national finals. They must impress the formidable panel of Andi Oliver, Matthew Fort and Oliver Peyton with their four course menus.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Andrew is attempting to pay tribute to NHS staff with his innovative take on a traditional healing recipe, `Mums Flu Fighting Chicken Soup. Jasons `A Tea From The Heart is a play on a post-war remedy which harks back to the era when the NHS was formed. And Chris is hoping to impress with his dish inspired by a therapeutic hospital garden featuring foraged herbs from the Welsh valley where he lives including wild chervil, pickled pine buds and yarrow tips.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: After the starter results, the chefs return to the kitchen hoping for high scores with their fish dishes. Chris once again is using ingredients he has foraged from around his restaurant, while Jason hopes to impress with a spicy scallop dish and Andrew prepares a cod dish with 11 different elements.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The chefs are pulling out all the stops in a bid for big scores with their mains. All three dishes pay tribute to the founder of the NHS Aneurin Bevan, Chriss `Everything But The Squeal is an ambitious pork sharing platter, while Jasons `For The Rich And The Poor showcases lamb, and Andrew is hoping to impress with `Aneurin Bevan For Better Steak.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It is the dessert course and the last opportunity for the chefs to impress. Chris is hoping to triumph with another dish which showcases the foraged ingredients available around his restaurant, while Jason attempts a technical feat in his dessert inspired by a childhood hospital stay and Andrew is inspired by a dish by his great grandmother used to cook. Which two will make it through to the judging chamber?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It is the Wales regional final as the two remaining chefs do everything they can to impress the judging panel of Andi Oliver, Matthew Fort and Oliver Peyton. After tensions ran high in the week, the chefs are under pressure to deliver perfection. The judges are joined by Aneira Thomas, the first baby born on the NHS, who is named in tribute to the services founder Aneurin Bevan and who went on to dedicate her life to the service. Who will win a place in the national finals?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its the turn of three chefs representing the South West, including two who have competed before but meet in the kitchen this week for the very first time.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its the fish course and with Tom renowned as a fish specialist all eyes are on him. Hes cooking his take on a classic British fish supper in tribute to a charity which looks after the health of local fishermen. Jude is attempting a complex fusion dish featuring four different fish and an unusual X Ray themed presentation, while Olivia is paying tribute to the staff who looked after her during the birth of her first son with her take on curried monkfish.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its the main course and two of the chefs are hoping duck will win the day. Tom is preparing a technically challenging duck dish steeped in NHS history while Jude is making a multi-element Chinese duck dish inspired by his mum. Olivia is hoping her dish, using two unusual cuts of beef, will score top points. Olivia also visits her local childrens hospital, home of the second busiest paediatric AandE in the country.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: With just one more chance to impress their veteran judge, the chefs are all hoping to use their pastry skills to deliver technically complex desserts. Toms cooking an `Invalid Fruit Tart inspired by a 1950s recipe, while Jude is making six different apple elements for his `An Apple A Day. Olivia is using stout in reference to an outdated old wives tale which suggested the drink was a healthy source of iron during pregnancy.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its time for the two remaining chefs representing the South West to go head to head as they cook their menus again for the exacting panel of Andi Oliver, Matthew Fort and Oliver Peyton. With a spot in the National Finals at stake, both are aiming for perfection as they bid for glory. The judges are joined by Barbara Childs, a Matron in the Cardiac Intensive Care Unit at Great Ormond Street Hospital, who isnt afraid to speak her mind in the chamber.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Three chefs take to the kitchen in a bid to represent the North West in the national final. They include Ellis Barrie, from the award winning Marram Grass in Anglesey, Liam Simpson-Trotman, an accomplished pastry chef and co-owner of the 4 AA rosette Orwells in Oxfordshire, and Craig Sherrington, who sharpened his skills working under top names including Eric Chavot and is now Chef Patron of Virginia House in Cumbria.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its the fish course and the chefs are all gunning for tens. Craig pays tribute to a friend who has revolutionised hospital food in Morecambe Bay, while Liam is inspired by health advice his grandma gave him and Ellis is dedicating his dish to the staff who delivered his son Albert. With cod, mackerel, shrimp and cockles on the menu, which of the chefs will score highly?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Time for the chefs to cook their mains and the pressure is on. Craig is hoping to impress using two cuts of Dexter beef, while Liam prepares his take on a Bajan lamb stew and Ellis prepares four cuts of suckling pig. Liam meets his personal hero, Nola Ishmael OBE, who became the UKs first ever black Director of Nursing.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its the dessert course and the last chance for the North West chefs to impress in a bid to make it through to the regional final. Liam is cooking `Not So Hot Toddy in honour of a friend who works nightshifts in the NHS, while Ellis `Worth Their Weight in Gold is dedicated to the staff who saved his brothers life and Craigs dish `The Missed Celebration pays tribute to his wife with an unusual Christmas concept.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two highest scoring chefs from the week return to cook their menus again. Judging them are Matthew Fort, Oliver Peyton and Andi Oliver who are joined by Dr Jake Dunning MBE, an infectious disease specialist who has been recognised for his work during the West Africa Ebola epidemic.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two highest scoring chefs from the week return to cook their menus again. Judging them are Matthew Fort, Oliver Peyton and Andi Oliver who are joined by Dr Jake Dunning MBE, an infectious disease specialist who has been recognised for his work during the West Africa Ebola epidemic.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Three chefs are doing battle to represent Northern Ireland. Tommy Heaney, Head Chef at the Great House Hotel in South Wales is taken on by Shauna Froydenlund, Chef Patron at Marcus Warings two Michelin starred restaurant and James Devine, the 2017 National Chef of the Year.

programme.title: Churchill: The Road to Victory
programme.desc: Winston Churchill served as the Prime Minister of Great Britain in arguably some of the most trying times of modern history. After Chamberlains resignation in 1940, Churchill was appointed during World War II and ultimately led the country down the road to victory.

programme.title: Edward III - Englands Greatest King
programme.desc: King Edward the Eighth of the United Kingdom, born in 1894, was raised as a future king.

programme.title: Henry V - Agincourt and The Hundred Years War
programme.desc: Henry V of England, born 1386, led during power struggles and the Hundred Years War.

programme.title: Farewell Israel: Bush, Iran and the Revolt of Islam
programme.desc: Filmmaker and Islamic history expert Joel Gilbert explores the history of the Muslim religion.

programme.title: Angels of the Sky
programme.desc: World War II pilots from the 8th Air Force share their harrowing experiences in POW camps after being shot down behind enemy lines, including their attempts to escape.

programme.title: Operation Carpetbagger: The Secret Mission to End World War II
programme.desc: Chronicling Operation Carpetbagger, one of the most top-secret missions of World War II.

programme.title: U-Boats: The Wolfpack/B-17: The Flying Fortress
programme.desc: A closer look at two of WW2s most terrifying and influential war machines.

programme.title: Dogfight Over Guadalcanal
programme.desc: The true story of two pilots, one American and the other Japanese, who confronted each other during a key WWII battle and, for some mysterious reason, did not shoot each other down.

programme.title: Hitlers Lost Battles
programme.desc: A look at the strategic decisions made by Hitler during the Battle of Britain, which led to Operation Barbarossa, and the Axis invasion of the Soviet Union.

programme.title: Hitlers Lost Battles
programme.desc: The poor military decisions made by Hitler started to become clear by 1942. America got involved in the conflict as a reaction to his declaration of war, as they made ground in North Africa.

programme.title: Remember Pearl Harbor Narrated by Tom Selleck
programme.desc: On December 7, 1941, Japanese planes dropped torpedoes and bombs on an American Pacific fleet at Pearl Harbor.

programme.title: The Bombing of Darwin
programme.desc: A look at the extraordinary events surrounding the strike on Darwin, Australia in 1942, in which Japanese forces killed more people and sunk more ships than at Pearl Harbor.

programme.title: Swordfish: World War IIs Surprising Aircraft And The Raid That Changed History
programme.desc: Fairey Swordfish was a tough WWII biplane, famed for torpedo attacks despite outdated design.

programme.title: The Rise of Napoleon Bonaparte A Military Prodigy
programme.desc: Napoleon Bonaparte, born in 1769 in Corsica, came from minor nobility and claimed Roman heritage.

programme.title: 1805: The Battle of Austerlitz
programme.desc: Recounting an important day in December of 1805, which became one of the most decisive battles and the greatest victory of the Napoleonic era.

programme.title: Al Murray: Why Does Everyone Hate the British Empire?
programme.desc: Al Murray pairs up with local comedians to explore the history of British colonial rule and its legacy. They find out about the origins of British rule, its impact and how its legacy continues to influence governance, culture, and attitudes.

programme.title: Shipwrecks: When History Resurfaces
programme.sub-title: Shipwrecked Sailors Of The Roman Empire
programme.desc: Kevin Sempe dives in search of wrecks harking back to the Roman Empires greatness. The journey begins with the Nave Romana.

programme.title: Invasion: The Normans
programme.desc: Following the course of the invasion, as Strongbow wins victory after victory. In 1171, Henry II himself arrives to takes control of Ireland, and grants land there to settlers from Britain. Now, archaeology and cutting-edge science reveal their fascinating lives.

programme.title: Medieval Dead
programme.desc: Carl Wark is a hill fort of unproven origin, but Bronze Age artefacts suggest a long occupation of the area. Tune in to see what the team can find.

programme.title: Medieval Dead
programme.desc: The siege of Vadum Jacob in 1179 saw Muslim forces overcome the Knights Templar. Now, forensic analysis of the battleground can shed light on this conquest.

programme.title: Medieval Dead
programme.desc: Forensic research and archaeology targets the condottieri, who were mercenary leaders contracted by Italian city-states and the Papacy during the Renaissance.

programme.title: Medieval Dead
programme.desc: Preserved swords from the Battle of Castillon in 1453 have been found on a river bank. What can these weapons tell us about the end of the Hundred Years War?

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: An end-terrace house in Alfreton in Derby bought by two brothers, a mid-terrace on the coast at Seaham in County Durham and a large three-storey property with a flat and shop in Taylorstown in the Rhondda Valley are all sold at auction. Martin Roberts, Lucy Alexander and Dion Dublin talk to the new owners to find out what they have planned for their auction buys.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts falls in love with a quirky cottage in Malvern bought by a builder and his wife, Dion Dublin talks to a first-time property developer who sees her end-terrace house in Chell in Stoke-on-Trent as something for her pension pot, and Lucy Alexander meets a divorced couple who have decided to form a new union as property developers doing up a shop and flat in Dover, Kent.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A large piece of land in Ratby in Leicestershire, a terrace in Chesterton in Staffordshire and a 1930s semi with a huge garden and huge potential are all sold at auction. Martin Roberts, Lucy Alexander and Dion Dublin meet the developers hoping to breathe new life into their auction buys.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A two-bedroom detached house in Basford in Nottingham, a top floor flat in Portsmouth and a large Edwardian terrace in Crewe are all sold under the hammer. Martin, Lucy and Dion meet the new owners and hear about their plans.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A multimillion-pound development in Farningham in Kent, an ambitious, total renovation project in Aspatria in Cumbria and a very small studio flat in Wallington in Surrey are all sold under the hammer. Martin and Lucy meet the new owners to discuss their plans.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A semi in Sutton Coldfield near Birmingham, a derelict house in a rural village near Dover in Kent and a basement without planning permission in Ipswich in Suffolk are all sold under the hammer.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A 20-year-old first-time developer, a banker turned developer and a builder who wants to create a new home for his soon-to-be-wife all buy properties at auction. Martin, Lucy and Dion are in Chesterton, Hornchurch and Sudbrook to hear their plans.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A run-down listed village house in Farningham in Kent is given the wow factor while houses in Great Barr in Birmingham and Crewe in Cheshire are also given great makeovers. Martin, Lucy and Dion meet the new owners and catch up on their progress after the transformations.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Lucy talks to a pastor and his wife who plan to build their dream eco house in Brompton in Kent. Meanwhile, Martin meets a first-time developer in Wolverhampton, and Dion looks at a huge building which already comes with a tenanted flat and a hair salon in Newcastle-under-Lyme.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A commercial and residential building in Beeston in Nottinghamshire, a four-storey house in East Dulwich in London and a flat in Godalming in Surrey are all given high-end makeovers. Will the developers, who all bought at auction, make a profit?

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A 1930s semi in Hatfield in Hertforshire, a flat in Croydon and a workshop and land in Heanor in Derbyshire are sold at auction. Martin, Lucy and Dion meet the owners and hear their plans to make money from their bricks and mortar.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A mum and dad buy a piece of land in Stevenage in Hertfordshire in the hope of developing a pair of houses for their children, a couple who buy a top-floor flat in Motherwell in Scotland want to make a profit on the rent, and two young brothers buy a run-down house in South Normanton.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A bungalow in Langton Green in Kent is given a high-end refurbishment and extension, a father-and-son team cleverly refigure the existing space in a terraced house in Stoke-on-Trent, and two lifelong friends have high hopes for a piece of land they buy behind a house they already own in Northwood in Middlesex.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A 1930s semi in Leamington Spa in Warwickshire, a terraced house in Londons Plumstead and a workshop on the Isle of Sheppey in Kent are all sold under the hammer. Martin Roberts, Lucy Alexander and Dion Dublin hear about the plans for the three properties.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts catches up on ambitious plans to redevelop the site of a former school in Ebbw Vale in Wales, while Dion Dublin talks to a first-time developer planning to extend up and out at a semi in Bloxwich in the West Midlands.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A flat in Sutton in London, a studio apartment in Chatham in Kent and a terraced house in Middlesbrough are all sold under the hammer. Martin, Lucy and Dion meet the new owners.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A bungalow in Biggin Hill with huge potential, a centuries-old cottage in a beautiful part of Devon and an end terrace crying out for an upstairs bathroom are all sold under the hammer. Martin, Lucy and Dion meet the new owners and hear their plans.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martel Maxwell joins the team, and looks at an old mews house in an historic part of Litchfield in Staffordshire, while Martin Roberts assesses the potential of a bungalow in Hanley in Stoke-on-Trent.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martel Maxwell looks at a large terraced house in Dover in Kent with bags of potential. Martin Roberts is in Camborne in Cornwall viewing a house with lots of issues. Dion Dublin meets a couple who bought a flat in Woodford Green in Essex.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martel Maxwell is in Gillingham in Kent meeting a couple embarking on their first renovation development, Martin Roberts talks to a man with ambitious plans for a house in Londons Chingford, and Dion Dublin looks round a semi in Wolverhampton.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martel Maxwell hears development plans for a two-bedroom flat in Godalming in Surrey, Martin Roberts looks at a house in a terrible state of repair in Telford in Shropshire, and Dion Dublin sizes up the work needing to be done at a terraced house in Smethwick in the West Midlands.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A flat with great sea views in Margate in Kent, a bungalow in Whaley Thorns in Nottinghamshire and an end terrace in Longbridge in Birmingham are all sold under the hammer.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer

programme.title: On the Case With Paula Zahn
programme.desc: A murdered teenagers body mysteriously turns up in the rural Iowa countryside. Paula Zahn examines the smallest of clues, which may turn out to be the key to bringing her killer to justice.

programme.title: Mean Girl Murders
programme.desc: In the cutthroat world of Vegas showgirls, Debbie Flores Narvaez isnt afraid to ruffle feathers as she climbs to the top, but when Debbie goes missing, investigators must wonder if a jealous dancer or a romantic rival took her out.

programme.title: Mean Girl Murders
programme.desc: Minnesota teen mum Cheyenne Clough is over the hallway dramas and petty jealousies of high school. She finds acceptance in a new crowd by moving in with two gals and their boyfriends. But after Cheyenne gets her own beau, distrust leads to death.
